message:
I've never been the 'prepared' friend. I'm usually the one scrambling for a pen at the doctor's office or realizing I forgot my keys halfway down the driveway.

But last month when a pipe burst in our basement at 2 a.m., I actually *knew* where our important documents were. I had a flashlight within arm's reach. I knew who to call and had their number written down (not just hoping my phone would charge). My partner looked at me like I'd grown a second head.

"You... have a plan for this?" he asked, half amazed, half amused.

Turns out putting together a simple go-bag and keeping a few key things organized doesn't require turning into a full-time prepper. It just means being a tiny bit ahead of chaos, and honestly? That feeling of having your ducks in a *vague* row is surprisingly good.

Have you ever had one of those moments where being even a *little* prepared actually came through? What was it?
